intro: The most concrete split between Chargebee and Lago is the pricing model: Chargebee runs on hybrid pricing from $599/mo (Performance), with a free tier, while Lago uses freemium with paid plans from $1. That shapes how predictable your monthly spend is and how much of the cost sits in infrastructure versus per-seat or per-event fees. Beyond the headline, Chargebee carries medium lock-in (medium — deep integrations, underandwithon logic) and hybrid pricing from $599/mo (Performance), with a free tier; Lago carries low lock-in (open-source billing, self-hostable) and freemium with paid plans from $1. Chargebee is a fit when you are saas. Lago is a fit when you are saas. The honest trade-off: Chargebee is explicitly not for simple projects; Lago is explicitly not for simple projects. On the product side, Chargebee is described as Subscription and revenue management platform supporting SaaS, e-commerce, and box subscriptions with Stripe and Braintree, and Lago is described as Open-source metering and usage-based billing API supporting flat, tiered, volume, and percentage pricing models.
quick_take: Chargebee is for saas; Lago is for saas; decide on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Chargebee if its hybrid pricing model fits your budget shape better than Lago's freemium model, and you are saas.
verdict_b: Choose Lago if its freemium pricing model fits your budget shape better than Chargebee's hybrid model, and you are saas.

faq.a: Chargebee starts at $599/mo (Performance); Lago starts at $1. On entry price alone that is the comparison, but real cost scales with users, events or volume, which the entry prices do not capture. Check each vendor's full tier list before committing.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Chargebee to Lago?
faq.a: Migration from Chargebee to Lago is feasible but not trivial: Chargebee has medium lock-in (medium — deep integrations, underandwithon logic). Core data can move; integrations and workflow logic need rebuild. Allow for a parallel-run period.

On our scoring Chargebee rates 5/5 for developer experience versus 4/5 for Lago. That suggests day-to-day workflows (CLI, SDKs, docs) are smoother in Chargebee. Scores are directional; run a short spike on your own stack to confirm.

faq.q: Is Lago a good alternative to Chargebee?
faq.a: Lago targets the same best-fit audience as Chargebee (saas). Note that Lago is explicitly not for simple projects, so it only replaces Chargebee if you are outside that exclusion. The lock-in profile differs (medium vs low), which is the main thing to weigh.
